text.title: llamaindex Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for llamaindex as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using chunk_size=512 and top_k=2 (LlamaIndex defaults) in production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The defaults are too conservative: top_k=2 misses relevant context; 512 tokens is fine for precision but combine with top_k=5-10 for robust retrieval. Always tune aggressively based on eval metrics before launching.

failure-mode.description: Vague tool descriptions in RouterQueryEngine ("handles documents about everything").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The LLM router selects based on description match to the query. Vague descriptions cause misrouting. Each description must be specific enough that the router can distinguish which index to use for a given query type.

failure-mode.description: Using string-keyed context dictionaries to pass data between all steps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ctx.set/get with arbitrary string keys creates implicit coupling and makes step dependencies invisible. Define explicit Event fields for step outputs; use ctx only for values genuinely shared across multiple steps (e.g., original query).

failure-mode.description: Calling from_documents() on large corpora synchronously in a request handler.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: from_documents() is blocking and slow for large corpora. Embed documents asynchronously at ingestion time using async batch processing; never rebuild on request.

failure-mode.description: Passing metadata_extractor=None omission when unexpected API costs appear.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some LlamaIndex configurations call the LLM for metadata extraction by default. Disable with metadata_extractor=None unless title/keyword extraction is explicitly needed.
